frontText: Move the cursor **left, down, up, right** without arrow keys
backText:
```
h j k l
```

Left, down, up, right ŌĆö keeps your fingers on the home row.

[Left-right motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#left-right-motions)

frontText: Jump to the start of the **next word**
backText:
```
w
```

Takes a count: `3w` jumps three words. `W` treats punctuation as part of the word.

[Word motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#word-motions)

frontText: Jump to the start of the **previous word**
backText:
```
b
```

The backward counterpart of `w`.

[Word motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#word-motions)

frontText: Jump to the **end of the word**
backText:
```
e
```

Useful with operators: `ce` changes to the end of the word.

[Word motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#word-motions)

frontText: Jump to the **very start of the line** (column 0)
backText:
```
0
```

Goes to column 0 even if the line is indented.

[Left-right motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#left-right-motions)

frontText: Jump to the **first non-blank character** of the line
backText:
```
^
```

Usually what you want in indented code, unlike `0`.

[Left-right motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#left-right-motions)

frontText: Jump to the **end of the line**
backText:
```
$
```

Combines with operators: `d$` (or `D`) deletes to the end of the line.

[Left-right motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#left-right-motions)

frontText: Jump to the **first line** of the file
backText:
```
gg
```

With a count it jumps to that line: `1G` does the same.

[Up-down motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#up-down-motions)

frontText: Jump to the **last line** of the file
backText:
```
G
```

Mnemonic: Go to the end.

[Up-down motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#up-down-motions)

frontText: Jump to **line 42**
backText:
```
42G
```

`:42` and `42gg` do the same.

[Up-down motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#up-down-motions)

frontText: Jump to the **previous empty line** (paragraph up)
backText:
```
{
```

Quick way to hop up between functions or blocks separated by blank lines.

[Object motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#object-motions)

frontText: Jump to the **next empty line** (paragraph down)
backText:
```
}
```

Quick way to hop down between functions or blocks separated by blank lines.

[Object motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#object-motions)

frontText: Scroll **half a page down**
backText:
```
Ctrl-d
```

Mnemonic: down. Keeps more visual context than a full-page jump.

[Scrolling](https://vimhelp.org/scroll.txt.html)

frontText: Scroll **half a page up**
backText:
```
Ctrl-u
```

Mnemonic: up.

[Scrolling](https://vimhelp.org/scroll.txt.html)

frontText: Scroll a **full page down**
backText:
```
Ctrl-f
```

Mnemonic: forward.

[Scrolling](https://vimhelp.org/scroll.txt.html)

frontText: Scroll a **full page up**
backText:
```
Ctrl-b
```

Mnemonic: backward.

[Scrolling](https://vimhelp.org/scroll.txt.html)

frontText: Move the cursor to the **top of the visible screen**
backText:
```
H
```

Mnemonic: High. Moves the cursor, not the view.

[Various motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#various-motions)

frontText: Move the cursor to the **middle of the visible screen**
backText:
```
M
```

Mnemonic: Middle.

[Various motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#various-motions)

frontText: Move the cursor to the **bottom of the visible screen**
backText:
```
L
```

Mnemonic: Low.

[Various motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#various-motions)

frontText: **Center the current line** on the screen
backText:
```
zz
```

`zt` scrolls it to the top, `zb` to the bottom.

[Scrolling relative to cursor](https://vimhelp.org/scroll.txt.html#scroll-cursor)

frontText: Jump onto the **next occurrence of the character `x`** in the current line
backText:
```
fx
```

`Fx` jumps backward, `tx` stops just before the `x`. Combines with operators: `dfx`.

[f motion](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#f)

frontText: **Repeat** the last `f`/`F`/`t`/`T` character jump
backText:
```
;
```

Press repeatedly to keep hopping to further occurrences.

[; motion](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#%3B)

frontText: Repeat the last `f`/`F`/`t`/`T` character jump **in the opposite direction**
backText:
```
,
```

Went one `;` too far? `,` steps back.

[, motion](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#%2C)

frontText: Jump **back to where you were** before the last jump (search, `G`, `%`, ŌĆ”)
backText:
```
Ctrl-o
```

Mnemonic: older position in the jump list. Press repeatedly to go further back.

[Jump motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#jump-motions)

frontText: Jump **forward** again in the jump history (undo a `Ctrl-o`)
backText:
```
Ctrl-i
```

Same key as `Tab`. The counterpart of `Ctrl-o`.

[Jump motions](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#jump-motions)

frontText: Jump to the **position of the last edit**
backText:
```
`.
```

Backtick + dot: the `.` mark always holds the spot of the most recent change.

[Marks](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#%60.)

frontText: **Delete (cut)** the current line
backText:
```
dd
```

The line goes into the unnamed register ŌĆö `p` afterwards pastes it (move a line = `dd` + `p`).

[dd](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#dd)

frontText: Delete **3 lines** at once
backText:
```
3dd
```

Any count works ŌĆö `10dd` deletes ten lines.

[dd](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#dd)

frontText: Delete from the cursor **to the end of the line**
backText:
```
D
```

Shorthand for `d$`. `C` does the same but leaves you in Insert mode.

[D](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#D)

frontText: Insert a **new line below** and start typing
backText:
```
o
```

Mnemonic: open. Respects the surrounding auto-indentation.

[o](https://vimhelp.org/insert.txt.html#o)

frontText: Insert a **new line above** and start typing
backText:
```
O
```

Capital `o` ŌĆö opens the line above the cursor.

[O](https://vimhelp.org/insert.txt.html#O)

frontText: **Copy (yank)** the current line
backText:
```
yy
```

`3yy` yanks three lines. `yyp` duplicates the current line.

[yy](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#yy)

frontText: **Paste** the yanked/deleted lines **below** the current line
backText:
```
p
```

For line-wise yanks (`yy`, `dd`, `V`+`y`) the text is inserted on a new line below.

[p](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#p)

frontText: **Paste** the yanked/deleted lines **above** the current line
backText:
```
P
```

Capital `p` ŌĆö inserts before/above instead of after/below.

[P](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#P)

frontText: **Join** the line below onto the end of the current line
backText:
```
J
```

Inserts a single space at the join; `gJ` joins without the space; `3J` joins three lines.

[J](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#J)

frontText: **Select several lines and copy them after another line**
backText:
```
V  (extend with j/k)  y   ŌåÆ  move to target  ŌåÆ  p
```

Line-wise Visual select, yank, then paste below the target line. Use `d` instead of `y` to move instead of copy.

[Visual mode](https://vimhelp.org/visual.txt.html)

frontText: **Copy lines 5ŌĆō9 after line 20** with a single Ex command (no Visual mode)
backText:
```vim
:5,9t20
```

`:t` copies a range after the target line. `.` = current line, `$` = last line.

[:copy](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#%3At)

frontText: **Move lines 5ŌĆō9 after line 20** with a single Ex command
backText:
```vim
:5,9m20
```

Like `:t` but moves instead of copying.

[:move](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#%3Am)

frontText: Search for the **word under the cursor** (forward)
backText:
```
*
```

Matches whole words only. `n`/`N` step through the results; `g*` matches partial words too.

[star](https://vimhelp.org/pattern.txt.html#star)

frontText: Search for the **word under the cursor backward**
backText:
```
#
```

The backward counterpart of `*`.

[#](https://vimhelp.org/pattern.txt.html#%23)

frontText: **Change the next match** of the last search (the key to plugin-free multi-cursor editing)
backText:
```
cgn
```

`gn` selects the next search match, `c` changes it. Type the replacement, press `Esc` ŌĆö then `.` repeats it on further matches.

[gn](https://vimhelp.org/visual.txt.html#gn)

frontText: **Repeat the last change** (e.g. apply a `cgn` replacement to the next match)
backText:
```
.
```

The dot command replays the last change. After `* cgn ŌĆ” Esc`: press `.` for each next occurrence, `n` to skip one.

[Repeating](https://vimhelp.org/repeat.txt.html#single-repeat)

frontText: Start a **column (block) selection**
backText:
```
Ctrl-v
```

Selects a rectangle of text; extend with `h j k l`, `fx`, `$` ŌĆö the basis of all column editing.

[Visual Block mode](https://vimhelp.org/visual.txt.html#visual-block)

frontText: With a block selected: **insert the same text at the left edge of every line** (e.g. comment out with `// `)
backText:
```
I
```

Type the text, press `Esc` ŌĆö it appears on all selected lines at once. Example: `Ctrl-v jjj I// Esc`.

[v_b_I](https://vimhelp.org/visual.txt.html#v_b_I)

frontText: With a block selected: **append the same text after the right edge of every line**
backText:
```
A
```

Type the text, press `Esc`. After extending the block with `$`, `A` appends at each line's own end even when lengths differ.

[v_b_A](https://vimhelp.org/visual.txt.html#v_b_A)

frontText: Extend a block selection to **the end of every line** (ragged right, different lengths)
backText:
```
$
```

Inside Visual Block, `$` makes the block follow each line's actual end ŌĆö then `A` appends there.

[v_b_$ ](https://vimhelp.org/visual.txt.html#v_b_%24)

frontText: With a block selected: **replace the selected column with new text on every line**
backText:
```
c
```

Type the replacement once, press `Esc` ŌĆö applied to all lines.

[v_b_c](https://vimhelp.org/visual.txt.html#v_b_c)

frontText: With a block selected: **delete the selected column** on every line
backText:
```
d
```

`x` does the same in Visual Block mode.

[v_d](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#v_d)

frontText: With a block selected: **replace every selected character with `x`**
backText:
```
rx
```

`r` + any character overwrites the whole selection with that character.

[v_r](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#v_r)

frontText: Turn a selected column of identical numbers into an **increasing sequence** (1, 2, 3, ŌĆ”)
backText:
```
g Ctrl-a
```

Adds 1 to the first number, 2 to the second, 3 to the thirdŌĆ” Plain `Ctrl-a` adds 1 to every number instead.

[v_g_CTRL-A](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#v_g_CTRL-A)

frontText: **Indent** the current line one level
backText:
```
>>
```

`3>>` indents three lines; the width comes from `shiftwidth`.

[>>](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#%3E%3E)

frontText: **Un-indent** the current line one level
backText:
```
<<
```

The counterpart of `>>`.

[<<](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#%3C%3C)

frontText: **Indent a visual selection** one level
backText:
```
>
```

`<` un-indents. The selection collapses afterwards ŌĆö `gv` reselects it, or press `.` to indent again.

[v_>](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#v_%3E)

frontText: **Re-select the last visual selection** (e.g. to indent it again)
backText:
```
gv
```

Restores the previous Visual selection exactly as it was.

[gv](https://vimhelp.org/visual.txt.html#gv)

frontText: **Auto-indent the current line** according to the file type's rules
backText:
```
==
```

In Visual mode a single `=` auto-indents the selection.

[=](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#%3D)

frontText: **Re-indent the whole file**
backText:
```
gg=G
```

Go to the top (`gg`), auto-indent (`=`) to the end (`G`).

[=](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#%3D)

frontText: **Indent the current line while typing** (Insert mode)
backText:
```
Ctrl-t
```

Works from anywhere in the line without leaving Insert mode.

[i_CTRL-T](https://vimhelp.org/insert.txt.html#i_CTRL-T)

frontText: **Un-indent the current line while typing** (Insert mode)
backText:
```
Ctrl-d
```

The Insert-mode counterpart of `Ctrl-t`.

[i_CTRL-D](https://vimhelp.org/insert.txt.html#i_CTRL-D)

frontText: Make Vim insert **spaces instead of tab characters**
backText:
```vim
set expandtab
```

Pair with `shiftwidth=4`, `tabstop=4`, `softtabstop=4` in `~/.vimrc` for consistent 4-space indentation.

['expandtab'](https://vimhelp.org/options.txt.html#%27expandtab%27)

frontText: **Convert the existing tabs/spaces** in the file to match your current settings
backText:
```vim
:retab
```

With `expandtab` set, this converts all tab characters to spaces.

[:retab](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#%3Aretab)

frontText: **Paste from the system clipboard**
backText:
```
"+p
```

The `+` register is the OS clipboard (needs Neovim or Vim with `+clipboard`). Never causes the indent "staircase" that terminal paste does.

[quoteplus](https://vimhelp.org/gui_x11.txt.html#quoteplus)

frontText: **Copy the selection to the system clipboard**
backText:
```
"+y
```

Select first (e.g. `V` + motions), then `"+y` puts it in the OS clipboard.

[Registers](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#registers)

frontText: Command to run **before pasting from the terminal** so auto-indent doesn't mangle the code (classic Vim)
backText:
```vim
:set paste
```

Paste, then `:set nopaste` to restore. Modern Vim/Neovim with bracketed paste usually makes this unnecessary.

['paste'](https://vimhelp.org/options.txt.html#%27paste%27)

frontText: **Paste and adjust the indentation** to match the current line
backText:
```
]p
```

Like `p`, but re-indents the pasted lines to the current context.

[]p](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#%5Dp)

frontText: Make `y`, `d` and `p` use the **system clipboard automatically**
backText:
```vim
set clipboard=unnamedplus
```

With this in `~/.vimrc`, no `"+` prefix is ever needed.

['clipboard'](https://vimhelp.org/options.txt.html#%27clipboard%27)

frontText: Jump to the **matching parenthesis / bracket / brace**
backText:
```
%
```

Works on `( ) [ ] { }`; the cursor may be on the bracket or before it on the line. `:packadd! matchit` (ships with Vim) extends it to HTML tags and `if/endif`.

[%](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#%25)

frontText: **Delete everything inside** the parentheses the cursor is in
backText:
```
di(
```

Works from anywhere inside the pair. Same pattern for `i{` `i[` `i"` `i'` `it`.

[Text objects](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#text-objects)

frontText: **Change everything inside** the parentheses (delete + enter Insert mode)
backText:
```
ci(
```

`ci"` and `ci'` do the same inside quotes ŌĆö the fastest way to replace an argument list or string.

[Text objects](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#text-objects)

frontText: **Copy everything inside** the parentheses
backText:
```
yi(
```

Yank the inner content without the parens themselves.

[Text objects](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#text-objects)

frontText: **Delete the parentheses and everything inside them**
backText:
```
da(
```

`a` = around (includes the pair), `i` = inner (contents only).

[Text objects](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#text-objects)

frontText: **Visually select the body** of the current `{ }` block
backText:
```
vi{
```

`va{` includes the braces. Pressing `i{` again expands to the enclosing block.

[Text objects](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#text-objects)

frontText: Jump to the **beginning of the current code block** (the unmatched `{` above)
backText:
```
[{
```

Jumps to the start of the enclosing function or `if` block; `[(` does the same for parens.

[[{](https://vimhelp.org/motion.txt.html#%5B%7B)

frontText: Jump to the **end of the current code block** (the unmatched `}` below)
**Search forward** for a pattern
backText:
```
/pattern
```

Press `Enter`, then `n` for the next match. `set ignorecase smartcase` makes it case-insensitive unless the pattern has a capital.

[/](https://vimhelp.org/pattern.txt.html#%2F)

frontText: **Search backward** for a pattern
backText:
```
?pattern
```

Like `/` but upward.

[?](https://vimhelp.org/pattern.txt.html#%3F)

frontText: Jump to the **next search match**
backText:
```
n
```

Repeats the last search in the same direction.

[n](https://vimhelp.org/pattern.txt.html#n)

frontText: Jump to the **previous search match**
backText:
```
N
```

Repeats the last search in the opposite direction.

[N](https://vimhelp.org/pattern.txt.html#N)

frontText: **Clear the search highlighting**
backText:
```vim
:noh
```

Short for `:nohlsearch` ŌĆö the highlights come back on the next search.

[:nohlsearch](https://vimhelp.org/pattern.txt.html#%3Anohlsearch)

frontText: Replace **all occurrences** of `old` with `new` in the whole file
backText:
```vim
:%s/old/new/g
```

`%` = every line, `g` = all matches per line.

[:substitute](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#%3Asubstitute)

frontText: Replace all occurrences in the file, **asking for confirmation** at each match
backText:
```vim
:%s/old/new/gc
```

At each match: `y` yes, `n` no, `a` all remaining, `q` quit.

[:s_flags](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#%3As_flags)

frontText: Replace only **within lines 5ŌĆō20**
backText:
```vim
:5,20s/old/new/g
```

Any range works before `:s` ŌĆö e.g. `.,+5` for the current and next five lines.

[:substitute](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#%3Asubstitute)

frontText: Replace only **inside the current visual selection**
backText:
```vim
:'<,'>s/old/new/g
```

Select with `V` first ŌĆö pressing `:` inserts the `'<,'>` range automatically.

[:substitute](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#%3Asubstitute)

frontText: Replace only **whole-word matches** of `old` (leave `older` and `bold` alone)
backText:
```vim
:%s/\<old\>/new/g
```

`\<` and `\>` anchor to word boundaries ŌĆö the same anchors `*` adds automatically.

[/\<](https://vimhelp.org/pattern.txt.html#%2F%5C%3C)

frontText: **Search a pattern across all project files** with Vim's built-in grep
backText:
```vim
:vimgrep /old/ **/*.js
```

Fills the quickfix list. `:grep -r old src/` uses the external grep instead (`set grepprg=rg\ --vimgrep` for ripgrep).

[:vimgrep](https://vimhelp.org/quickfix.txt.html#%3Avimgrep)

frontText: **Open the list of search results** after `:vimgrep`/`:grep`
backText:
```vim
:copen
```

Opens the quickfix window; `Enter` on a line jumps to that match.

[:copen](https://vimhelp.org/quickfix.txt.html#%3Acopen)

frontText: Jump to the **next / previous quickfix match**
backText:
```vim
:cnext    (and :cprev)
```

Steps through the `:vimgrep`/`:grep` results file by file.

[:cnext](https://vimhelp.org/quickfix.txt.html#%3Acnext)

frontText: After a project-wide `:grep`/`:vimgrep`: **run a replacement in every matched file and save**
backText:
```vim
:cfdo %s/old/new/g | update
```

`:cfdo` runs the command in each file in the quickfix list; `| update` writes each changed file.

[:cfdo](https://vimhelp.org/quickfix.txt.html#%3Acfdo)

frontText: Rename the variable under the cursor in the **whole file** without retyping its name (after pressing `*`)
backText:
```vim
:%s//newName/g
```

An **empty pattern** reuses the last search ŌĆö and `*` already added whole-word anchors, so `count` won't match `counter`.

[:substitute](https://vimhelp.org/change.txt.html#%3Asubstitute)

frontText: Jump to the **local definition** of the variable under the cursor
backText:
```
gd
```

Mnemonic: goto definition. `gD` searches from the start of the file (global definition).

[gd](https://vimhelp.org/pattern.txt.html#gd)

frontText: **Toggle between the two files** you edited most recently
backText:
```
Ctrl-^
```

Also written `Ctrl-6`. Bounces between the current and the alternate buffer ŌĆö perfect for code + test.

[CTRL-^](https://vimhelp.org/editing.txt.html#CTRL-%5E)

frontText: **List all open buffers**
backText:
```vim
:ls
```

Shows buffer numbers and names; `%` marks the current, `#` the alternate buffer.

[:ls](https://vimhelp.org/windows.txt.html#%3Als)

frontText: **Switch to an open buffer by a fragment of its name**
backText:
```vim
:b part
```

Matches any substring ŌĆö `:b ctrl` jumps to `user_controller.js`. `Tab` cycles through multiple matches.

[:buffer](https://vimhelp.org/windows.txt.html#%3Abuffer)

frontText: Switch to the **next buffer**
backText:
```vim
:bn
```

Short for `:bnext`; wraps around at the end of the buffer list.

[:bnext](https://vimhelp.org/windows.txt.html#%3Abnext)

frontText: Switch to the **previous buffer**
backText:
```vim
:bp
```

Short for `:bprevious`.

[:bprevious](https://vimhelp.org/windows.txt.html#%3Abprevious)

frontText: **Close (delete) the current buffer** without quitting Vim
backText:
```vim
:bd
```

Short for `:bdelete` ŌĆö the file stays on disk, only the buffer is unloaded.

[:bdelete](https://vimhelp.org/windows.txt.html#%3Abdelete)

frontText: **Open a file by path** (with Tab completion)
backText:
```vim
:e path/to/file
```

Short for `:edit`; `Tab` completes names (`set wildmenu` gives a visual menu). `:e!` reloads the current file discarding changes.

[:edit](https://vimhelp.org/editing.txt.html#%3Aedit)

frontText: **Fuzzy-find and open a deeply nested file** in stock Vim (no plugins)
backText:
```vim
:find usrctl<Tab>
```

Requires in `~/.vimrc`: `set path+=**` (search recursively), `set wildmenu`, `set wildoptions=fuzzy` (Vim 9 / Neovim) ŌĆö then a few characters match `src/user_controller.js`.

[:find](https://vimhelp.org/editing.txt.html#%3Afind)
